{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8699","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8699/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8699/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8699/events","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8699","id":2615744026,"node_id":"I_kwDOBFKgkM6b6RYa","number":8699,"title":"Create NCCL Communicator with 2 different NIC.","user":{"login":"Lssyes","id":62103375,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYyMTAzMzc1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/62103375?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Lssyes","html_url":"https://github.com/Lssyes","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Lssyes/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Lssyes/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Lssyes/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Lssyes/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Lssyes/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Lssyes/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Lssyes/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Lssyes/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Lssyes/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":472944224,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw0NzI5NDQyMjQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/labels/cat:bug","name":"cat:bug","color":"ee0701","default":false,"description":"Bugs"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-26T10:37:00Z","updated_at":"2024-10-26T10:37:00Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

Hi,

I have two nodes (each with 1 GPU, and both IB + Ethernet NICs). I'm trying to create two communicators, one for IB and one for Ethernet, respectively. I use environment variables to control the creation of each communicator. However, the environment variable changes do not work when I try to create the second communicator on the second NIC (regardless of whether the second NIC is Ethernet or IB0). The environment variable changes only seem to affect the first communicator.

I suspect this issue may be related to how Python or Ray manages environment variables. To verify this, I attempted the same operation using native NCCL in a C program. In the C code, I was able to create the desired communicator by setting the appropriate environment variables with the putenv function before calling ncclCommInitRank. Alternatively, I could achieve the same result using ncclCommInitRankConfig. However, these functionalities are restricted in CuPy, so I implemented a wrapper for ncclCommInitRankConfig in CuPy 10.6.0, as shown in the code below. Despite this effort, I still cannot create two communicators on different NICs simultaneously using CuPy.

This situation has left me quite confused, hope someone can help me with this!!!

### To Reproduce

```py
import ray
from cupy.cuda import nccl
import cupy as cp
import time
import os

@ray.remote(num_gpus=1)
class GPUHost:
    def __init__(self, ray_rank, nccl_uuid_list):
        self.ray_rank = ray_rank
        
        self.nccl_uuid_list = nccl_uuid_list
        self.comms = [None] * 100
        self.comm_args = [None] * 100
        print(cp.cuda.runtime.getDeviceCount())
        
    def init_communication(self, is_send, idx, set_ib):
        my_rank = 0 if is_send else 1
        peer_rank = 1 if is_send else 0
        
        # print(f"Rank {self.ray_rank} init communication nccl_uuid: {self.nccl_uuid_list[idx]}")
        config = cp.cuda.nccl.NcclConfig()
        
        if set_ib:
            config.netName = "IB"
            comm = cp.cuda.nccl.NcclCommunicator(2, self.nccl_uuid_list[idx], my_rank, config)
        else:
            print("Socket")
            config.netName = "Socket"
            comm = cp.cuda.nccl.NcclCommunicator(2, self.nccl_uuid_list[idx], my_rank, config)
            
        if comm is None:
            print("Failed to create communicator")
            exit(1)
        self.comms[idx] = comm
        self.comm_args[idx] = (my_rank, peer_rank)
        
        
    def profile_send_recv(self, buffer, is_send, idx):
        if is_send:
            _, peer_rank = self.comm_args[idx]
            self.comms[idx].send(buffer.data.ptr, buffer.size, cp.cuda.nccl.NCCL_FLOAT32, 
                                 peer_rank, cp.cuda.Stream.null.ptr)
        else:
            _, peer_rank = self.comm_args[idx]
            self.comms[idx].recv(buffer.data.ptr, buffer.size, cp.cuda.nccl.NCCL_FLOAT32,
                                 peer_rank, cp.cuda.Stream.null.ptr)
        
    def profile(self, is_send, idx, set_ib):
        size = 32 * 1024 * 1024
        repeat = 10
        
        # 改环境变量不生效啊, 可能是ray的原因
        # if idx == 1:
        #     os.environ["NCCL_IB_DISABLE"] = "1"
        self.init_communication(is_send, idx, set_ib)
        cp.cuda.Device(0).synchronize()
        
        buffer = cp.ones(size, dtype=cp.float32)
        for _ in range(3):
            self.profile_send_recv(buffer, is_send, idx)
        cp.cuda.Device(0).synchronize()
            
        start = time.time()
        for _ in range(repeat): 
            self.profile_send_recv(buffer, is_send, idx)
        cp.cuda.Device(0).synchronize()
        elapsed_time = time.time() - start
        
        ## calculate bandwidth
        GB = 1024 * 1024 * 1024
        bandwidth = size * 4 * repeat / GB / elapsed_time
        
        print(f"Rank {self.ray_rank} {'send' if is_send else 'recv'} bandwidth: {bandwidth} GB/s")
        
        
        
                
        print(f"Rank {self.ray_rank} finished")
        
        
if __name__ == "__main__":
    ray.init()
    nodes = ["ip5", "ip4", "ip1006"]
    nccl_uuid_list = [cp.cuda.nccl.get_unique_id() for _ in range(100)]
    env_vars = {
        "NCCL_DEBUG": "VERSION",
        "NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME": "ib0",
        # "NCCL_IB_DISABLE": "1",
    }
    host_0 = GPUHost.options(runtime_env={"env_vars": env_vars}, 
                             resources={nodes[0]: 1}).remote(ray_rank=0, 
                                                             nccl_uuid_list=nccl_uuid_list)
    host_1 = GPUHost.options(runtime_env={"env_vars": env_vars}, 
                             resources={nodes[1]: 1}).remote(ray_rank=1, 
                                                             nccl_uuid_list=nccl_uuid_list)
    # host_2 = GPUHost.options(runtime_env={"env_vars": env_vars}, 
    #                          resources={nodes[2]: 1}).remote(ray_rank=2, 
    #                                                          nccl_uuid_list=nccl_uuid_list)

        
    task_2 = [host_1.profile.remote(True, 1, set_ib=False), host_0.profile.remote(False, 1, set_ib=False)]
    ray.get(task_2)
    time.sleep(3)
    print("--------------------")
    
    
    task_1 = [host_0.profile.remote(True, 0, set_ib=True), host_1.profile.remote(False, 0, set_ib=True)]
    ray.get(task_1)
    


    
    # ray.shutdown()
    
    

```



```pyx
# cupy/cupy_backends/cuda/libs/nccl.pyx

cdef extern from "stdlib.h":
    int setenv(const char* name, const char* value, int overwrite)
    int putenv(const char* string)

cdef extern from "string.h":
    char* strdup(const char* s)
    
cdef class NcclConfig:
    cdef ncclConfig_t _config
    cdef bytes _netName_bytes

    def __cinit__(self):
        self._config.blocking = 1
        self._config.cgaClusterSize = 0
        self._config.minCTAs = 1
        self._config.maxCTAs = 32
        self._config.netName = NULL
        self._config.splitShare = 0
        self._netName_bytes = None

    property blocking:
        def __get__(self):
            return self._config.blocking
        def __set__(self, value):
            self._config.blocking = value

    property cgaClusterSize:
        def __get__(self):
            return self._config.cgaClusterSize
        def __set__(self, value):
            self._config.cgaClusterSize = value

    property minCTAs:
        def __get__(self):
            return self._config.minCTAs
        def __set__(self, value):
            self._config.minCTAs = value

    property maxCTAs:
        def __get__(self):
            return self._config.maxCTAs
        def __set__(self, value):
            self._config.maxCTAs = value

    property netName:
        def __get__(self):
            if self._config.netName is NULL:
                return None
            else:
                return self._netName_bytes.decode()
        def __set__(self, value):
            if value is None:
                self._config.netName = NULL
                self._netName_bytes = None
            else:
                self._netName_bytes = value.encode()
                self._config.netName = self._netName_bytes

    property splitShare:
        def __get__(self):
            return self._config.splitShare
        def __set__(self, value):
            self._config.splitShare = value



···
cdef class NcclCommunicator:
    cdef:
        ncclComm_t _comm

    def __cinit__(self):
        self._comm = <ncclComm_t>0

    def __init__(self, int ndev, tuple commId, int rank,
                 NcclConfig config=None):
        cdef char* env_var

        if config is not None:     
            if config.netName == "Socket":
                env_var = strdup(b"NCCL_IB_DISABLE=1")
                putenv(env_var)
                print("set env: NCCL_IB_DISABLE=1")
            elif config.netName == "IB":
                env_var = strdup(b"NCCL_IB_DISABLE=0")
                putenv(env_var)
                print("set env: NCCL_IB_DISABLE=0")
            else:
                raise ValueError("Invalid netName: {}".format(config.netName))

        
        cdef ncclUniqueId _uniqueId
        assert len(commId) == NCCL_UNIQUE_ID_BYTES
        for i in range(NCCL_UNIQUE_ID_BYTES):
            _uniqueId.internal[i] = commId[i]
        with nogil:
            if config is None:
                status = ncclCommInitRank(&self._comm, ndev, _uniqueId, rank)
            else:
                status = ncclCommInitRankConfig(&self._comm, ndev, _uniqueId, rank, &config._config)
        check_status(status)
```
